deploy-and-notify: Vercel deploy hooks return immediately with a deployment ID — you must poll the deployment status endpoint until state is READY or ERROR before posting to Discord, or you will notify with an incomplete status.

Discord bot tokens require the MESSAGE_CONTENT privileged intent only for reading messages; sending to a channel requires the bot to have Send Messages permission AND be present in the server — a missing guild membership returns a 403 that looks like a permissions error.

GitHub push webhooks for monorepos fire on every push regardless of path — if you only want main-branch production deploys, filter on ref === 'refs/heads/main' in your handler or you will trigger deploys from feature branches.
pr-review-notify: GitHub pull_request webhooks fire for opened, synchronize, reopened, closed, and more — filter on action === 'opened' only, or every force-push will re-trigger the notification and create duplicate Linear tasks.

Linear labels are identified by name within a team scope, but the API requires the label UUID — query the team's labels first to resolve 'code-review' to its ID, or issue creation will fail silently with a null label.

GitHub's secondary rate limit on the Search API is 30 requests/minute — if this recipe runs alongside other GitHub automations, the webhook diff-stats fetch can get throttled and return a 403 with a Retry-After header.
ai-code-review: GitHub PR diffs can exceed 100KB for large refactors — the Anthropic API will reject inputs over the model's context window. Truncate the diff to changed hunks only (skip lockfiles, generated code) and cap at ~8000 tokens.

Posting a review comment via the GitHub Issues Comments API has a 65,536-character body limit — if the Anthropic response is very long, split it into multiple comments or use the Pull Request Reviews API for inline comments.
The Anthropic API requires an explicit max_tokens parameter — omitting it returns an error, unlike OpenAI which defaults to model max. Always set max_tokens in the request.
user-onboarding-saas: Stripe sends duplicate webhook events during retries — use the checkout session ID as an idempotency key when creating the Supabase user, or you will get unique constraint violations on email.

Supabase RLS policies apply even to the service_role key unless you explicitly bypass them — if user creation fails with a 'permission denied' error, check that RLS policies allow inserts from the service role or use the admin API.
Resend requires domain verification (DNS TXT + DKIM records) before sending — emails from unverified domains are silently dropped, so test with a verified domain before going live.
Stripe amounts are in cents (integer) but your display and Supabase storage likely expect decimal — divide by 100 before storing or notifying, or plan metadata will show '2999' instead of '$29.99'.
scheduled-report: Supabase RPC/SQL queries respect Row Level Security by default — use the service_role key (not anon key) for analytics queries, or the query will return zero rows if no user context is set.

OpenAI rate limits vary by tier — on Tier 1 accounts, gpt-4o is limited to ~500 RPM and 30K TPM. If the analytics payload is large, you may hit token-per-minute limits. Summarize raw data before sending to the LLM.
Notion API blocks have a 2000-block limit per request and rich text blocks max at 2000 characters each — a long AI-generated report must be chunked into multiple append calls.
Slack messages over 4000 characters are truncated in the preview — use Block Kit with multiple section blocks, or post a summary and thread the full report as a follow-up.
new-signup-automation: Discord DMs require the bot to share a server with the user AND the user must have DMs enabled — matching by email is not a native Discord operation. You need a prior mapping of email-to-Discord-user-ID, or fall back to posting a welcome in a public channel.
Firebase createUser will throw auth/email-already-exists if the Stripe webhook retries — wrap the call in a try-catch and treat 'already exists' as a success, then update custom claims.

Mailchimp's PUT member endpoint (upsert) requires the subscriber hash (MD5 of lowercase email) in the URL path — using the raw email in the path returns a 404. Always lowercase and MD5-hash the email.

Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook signatures use the raw request body — if your middleware parses JSON before verification, the signature check will fail. Use the raw body buffer for verification.
deploy-monitoring: Vercel fires deployment.created, deployment.ready, and deployment.error events — subscribe only to deployment.ready and deployment.error, or your health check will hit a URL that is still building and return a false-negative 502.

New Vercel deployments use unique preview URLs (e.g., project-abc123.vercel.app) that differ from the production domain — health-check the actual deployment URL, not the production alias, to test the specific deploy.
Grafana Cloud's annotation API requires the dashboard numeric ID (not the UID slug) — use the dashboard search API to resolve the slug to an ID first, or annotations will 404.
invoice-automation: Stripe amounts are in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD, yen for JPY) — Airtable and Resend templates expect human-readable amounts. Divide by 100 for cent-based currencies, but NOT for zero-decimal currencies like JPY.

Stripe can send its own invoice emails by default — if you do not disable Stripe's automatic emails in Dashboard > Settings > Emails, customers will receive duplicate invoices (one from Stripe, one from Resend).
Airtable API rate limit is 5 requests per second per base — if multiple invoices finalize in a burst (e.g., subscription renewal day), queue writes or use batch create (up to 10 records per call).
Resend email templates must be created in the dashboard before the API can reference them — a missing template_id returns a 422 error that does not clearly indicate the template is missing.
security-alert-pipeline: Dependabot severity levels (critical, high, medium, low) do not directly map to Linear priority numbers (1=urgent, 2=high, 3=medium, 4=low) — build an explicit mapping or critical alerts will be created with the wrong priority.

GitHub Dependabot webhooks require the repository to have Dependabot alerts enabled AND the webhook must subscribe to the 'dependabot_alert' event type — this is a separate event from 'security_advisory'.

Linear's GraphQL API uses UUIDs for team_id and label IDs, not human-readable names — resolve 'security' label name to its UUID via the labels query before creating the issue, or the mutation will fail.

Notion select properties must use pre-defined options — if you pass a Severity value that does not exist as a select option in the database, the API returns a validation error. Pre-create all four severity options.
multi-cloud-deploy: Vercel and Netlify deploy hooks both return immediately and require polling for completion — if you notify Discord before both are done, the status will be 'building' not 'ready'. Poll both with independent timeouts (Vercel ~10min, Netlify ~5min).

Vercel environment variables are scoped per-project and per-environment (production/preview/development) — if the frontend deploy fails with missing env vars, check that production-scoped variables are set, not just preview.
Discord message embeds have a 6000-character total limit across all fields — if both deploy logs are included, truncate build output to essential lines only.

GitHub push webhooks include a 'modified_files' list only for the head commit — for force-pushes or squash merges, the list may be incomplete. Use the Compare API to get the full diff between before/after SHAs.
open-source-triage: Anthropic API JSON mode is not guaranteed to return valid JSON — always wrap the response parse in try-catch. Use a system prompt with explicit JSON schema and few-shot examples to improve reliability.

GitHub label application requires the labels to already exist in the repository — if the AI suggests a label like 'performance' that does not exist, the API returns 422. Pre-create expected labels or create them via API before applying.

GitHub API rate limit is 5000 requests/hour with a token, but popular open-source repos can receive hundreds of issues per day — batch webhook processing and use conditional requests (If-None-Match) to conserve quota.
Discord role mentions (@maintainers) require the bot to have the Mention Everyone permission or the role to be set as mentionable — otherwise the mention renders as plain text with no notification.
github-linear-slack-dev-flow: Linear issue linking depends on the branch name containing the issue ID (e.g., 'ENG-123-fix-bug') — if developers use non-standard branch names, the PR-to-issue lookup fails. Document the naming convention or parse from PR body.
Linear's GraphQL API uses cursor-based pagination (Relay style) — if you query issues by identifier and get no result, check that you are using the correct team prefix (e.g., ENG, not ENGINEERING).

GitHub pull_request webhooks fire for multiple actions (opened, synchronize, review_requested, closed) — map each action to the correct Linear status transition, or status will only update on open, not on merge.
figma-github-vercel-design-deploy: Figma webhooks must be registered via the Figma REST API (POST /v2/webhooks) — there is no UI for this. The webhook secret is returned only once at creation time; store it immediately for signature verification.

Vercel preview deployments require a branch ref, not a commit SHA — if the GitHub issue creation step does not also create a branch, the Vercel deploy step has no ref to build. Automate branch creation from the issue.
Figma file_key is extracted from the URL (figma.com/file/{file_key}/...) and is case-sensitive — passing a malformed key returns a 404 from the Figma API with no helpful error message.
Vercel preview URLs are ephemeral and change with every deploy — do not store them as permanent links. Use the deployment's inspect URL or alias for stable references.
posthog-slack-linear-feature-flag-incide: PostHog action webhooks send a generic payload — error_rate and baseline_rate are not included by default. You must configure a PostHog Action with a webhook that includes custom properties from your event schema.

Linear priority 1 (Urgent) triggers immediate notifications to assignees — if no assignee is set, the urgent issue goes unnoticed. Always set a default assignee or use a team-level auto-assignment rule.

PostHog feature flag evaluation is eventually consistent — there can be a delay between flag rollout and error data appearing. Set a minimum observation window (e.g., 15 minutes) before triggering the alert to avoid false positives.

Slack messages with @channel or @here in the text field require the bot to have chat:write permission and the channel to allow bot mentions — otherwise the mention is silently stripped from the posted message.
datadog-slack-incident-alert: Datadog webhook payloads use template variables ($EVENT_MSG, $ALERT_METRIC, etc.) that must be configured in the Datadog webhook integration settings — the raw webhook sends minimal data unless you define a custom payload template.

Slack channel names must be lowercase, max 80 characters, no spaces or periods — dynamically generated names like 'incident-2026-04-10-payment-service' can exceed limits or contain invalid characters. Sanitize and truncate before creating.
Slack's conversations.create API requires the channels:manage scope, which is separate from chat:write — the bot can post messages but not create channels unless this additional scope is granted.
Datadog monitors can flap between OK and ALERT rapidly — add a recovery threshold or evaluation window to prevent creating multiple incident channels for the same issue within minutes.
bug-triage-ai: Sentry webhook payloads for issue events can be very large (>100KB with full stack traces) — if your handler has a body size limit (common in serverless), the webhook silently fails. Increase the limit or use Sentry's internal integration which sends compact payloads.

OpenAI's gpt-4o has a 128K context window but charges per token — sending full stack traces with 200+ frames is expensive and often unnecessary. Truncate to the top 50 frames and include only the exception chain.

Sentry groups similar errors into issues — a single webhook may represent hundreds of events. Check the issue frequency/count before creating a Linear task to avoid flooding the backlog with low-occurrence errors.

Linear priority field accepts integers (0=none, 1=urgent, 2=high, 3=medium, 4=low), not strings — passing Sentry's frequency as a string will cause the mutation to fail. Map frequency ranges to integer priorities explicitly.
incident-response: PagerDuty Events API v2 requires a routing_key (integration key), not the REST API token — using the wrong key type returns a 'routing key not found' error. Create a service-level Events API v2 integration in PagerDuty to get the correct key.

Jira priority names vary by instance — the default is 'Highest/High/Medium/Low/Lowest', not P1-P5. Query /rest/api/3/priority to get your instance's priority IDs before mapping from PagerDuty severity.
Grafana annotation timestamps must be in epoch milliseconds — PagerDuty's triggered_at is ISO 8601. Convert before calling the Grafana API or the annotation will be placed at Unix epoch (1970).
PagerDuty webhook V3 format nests incident data under event.data — V2 uses messages[].incident. If you configured V3 but parse V2 format, all fields will be undefined.
infrastructure-scaling: Cloudflare Analytics API (GraphQL) requires an API token with the Analytics:Read permission at the zone level — the Global API Key works but is a security risk. Use a scoped token.

PagerDuty Events API v2 requires a routing_key tied to a specific service — the 'service_id' in config must be the integration key from the service's Events API v2 integration, not the service's PagerDuty ID.

OpenAI token limits apply to both input and output — large Cloudflare analytics payloads (24 hours of per-minute data) can exceed context limits. Aggregate data to hourly buckets before sending to the LLM.
PagerDuty dedup_key should be deterministic for the same prediction window — use a hash of date + zone_id to prevent duplicate alerts. Without dedup, each cron run creates a new incident.
postgres-schema-audit: The postgresql-mcp server runs queries with the credentials of the configured database user — ensure this user has SELECT on information_schema.tables, information_schema.columns, and pg_catalog.pg_stat_user_tables, or the audit will return empty results.

Comparing this week's snapshot to last week's requires persisting the previous snapshot somewhere (config store, file, or database) — Slack messages are not reliable storage. Store snapshots in a dedicated table or object store.

Large databases with 200+ tables can produce schema snapshots exceeding the OpenAI context window (128K tokens for gpt-4o) — pre-filter to changed tables only or use a structured JSON diff before sending to the LLM.

pg_stat_statements and pg_stat_user_tables row counts are estimates from the planner — for exact counts, the query needs COUNT(*) per table, which can be slow on large tables. Use the estimate for audits and note the caveat.
qdrant-rag-knowledge-base: The Qdrant collection must be created with the correct vector dimension BEFORE upserting — text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dimensional vectors. A dimension mismatch returns an error that does not clearly state the cause.

GitHub's /contents API returns Base64-encoded file content and has a 1MB file size limit — for larger docs, use the Git Blobs API. Also, the API paginates directory listings at 1000 files; use the Git Trees API for larger doc sets.
OpenAI embedding API accepts up to 2048 inputs per batch call — but total tokens across all inputs must stay under 8191 tokens per input. Chunk documents before batching, not after.

Qdrant distance metric (Cosine, Euclid, Dot) is set at collection creation and cannot be changed — OpenAI embeddings are normalized, so Cosine and Dot produce identical rankings, but Euclid does not. Use Cosine for OpenAI embeddings.
vector-search-support-bot: The embedding model for queries MUST match the model used during indexing — if the knowledge base was built with text-embedding-3-small but queries use text-embedding-ada-002, similarity scores will be meaningless and results random.

Slack app_mention events require the Event Subscriptions feature enabled with the app_mentions:read scope — this is separate from bot token scopes. Missing this scope means the bot never receives mention events.

Qdrant score_threshold varies by distance metric — for Cosine similarity, 0.7 is a reasonable threshold, but for Dot product on unnormalized vectors, scores can exceed 1.0. Calibrate the threshold to your specific corpus.

Slack messages have a 4000-character limit for the text field — if the RAG answer plus citations exceeds this, the message silently truncates. Use Block Kit blocks or split into a summary message and threaded detail.
mongo-to-postgres-nightly-sync: MongoDB Atlas Data API has a 16MB response limit and a default timeout of 10 seconds — if the changed document set is large, paginate with skip/limit and add projection to fetch only needed fields.

Neon Postgres serverless connections auto-suspend after 5 minutes of inactivity — the first query after suspension has a ~1s cold start. For nightly syncs this is fine, but set a connection timeout > 5s to handle the wake-up.
MongoDB ObjectId fields do not directly map to Postgres types — store them as TEXT or UUID in the target table. Casting errors will cause the entire INSERT batch to fail with no partial commit.

The sync checkpoint (last_sync_ts) must be persisted outside of Slack — if the agent relies on parsing the last Slack message to find the checkpoint, message edits or deletions will break the sync. Store it in a config table or environment variable.
redis-cache-warmer: Upstash Redis REST API has a rate limit of ~1000 commands/second on the free tier — warming 500 keys with individual SET calls will take 500 requests. Use the pipeline endpoint (POST /pipeline) to batch all SETs in a single HTTP call.

The postgresql-mcp server is strictly read-only — do not attempt to update a 'last_warmed_at' timestamp through it. Use a separate write path or track cache warm status in Redis itself with a metadata key.

Microsoft Teams Graph API requires delegated or application permissions (ChannelMessage.Send) and an Azure AD app registration — this is significantly more setup than Slack webhooks. The access token expires every hour and must be refreshed.
TTL of 6 hours means the cache goes cold before next day's peak if the warmer runs too early — schedule the job 30-60 minutes before the expected traffic peak, not at midnight.
postgres-slow-query-alert: pg_stat_statements must be loaded via shared_preload_libraries in postgresql.conf and requires a server restart to enable — it cannot be loaded dynamically. If not present, the query returns 'relation pg_stat_statements does not exist'.

GitLab issue search is full-text and fuzzy — deduplication by queryid requires embedding the queryid as a unique token (e.g., '[queryid:12345]') in the issue body so search reliably finds exact matches, not partial ones.

pg_stat_statements query text is truncated to track_activity_query_size (default 1024 bytes) — long queries like ORM-generated JOINs will be cut off, making root cause analysis harder. Increase this setting if you need full query text.

GitLab project access tokens have an expiration date (max 1 year) — long-running agents using these tokens will silently fail after expiry. Set a calendar reminder or build a token rotation mechanism.
content-pipeline: Airtable API rate limit is 5 requests per second per base — if the editorial calendar is large and requires pagination, add delays between page fetches or you will get 429 errors with a 30-second backoff.
Anthropic API requires an explicit max_tokens parameter — if omitted, the request fails with a validation error. Set it based on expected article length (4000 tokens is roughly 3000 words).

Notion page content is structured as blocks, not plain text — the AI-generated markdown must be converted to Notion block format (paragraph, heading, bulleted_list_item, etc.) before creation, or the content appears as a single unformatted text blob.

Airtable field names are case-sensitive and space-sensitive — 'publish_date' and 'Publish Date' are different fields. Verify exact field names from the Airtable schema before building the filter formula.
customer-feedback-loop: OpenAI rate limits on gpt-4o vary by tier — sending a batch of 20 survey responses in a single prompt can exceed token-per-minute limits on lower tiers. Process in smaller batches of 5-10 responses if you hit 429 errors.

Airtable records must be marked as processed after analysis to prevent reprocessing — use a PATCH request to set the 'processed' checkbox immediately after the OpenAI call, not after the Slack post, to avoid reprocessing if Slack fails.
Slack messages over 4000 characters are truncated — sentiment analysis of 20+ responses will likely exceed this. Post a summary in the main message and use a thread for the per-response breakdown.

Airtable's filterByFormula syntax uses its own formula language (not SQL) — the filter 'processed=false' must be written as filterByFormula=NOT({processed}) or {processed}=FALSE() depending on field type.
meeting-followup: Calendly webhook payloads nest attendee data under invitee.questions_and_answers as an array of objects — each Q&A has 'question' and 'answer' fields, not a flat key-value map. Parse accordingly.

Slack's chat.scheduleMessage API requires the post_at timestamp in Unix epoch seconds and the time must be between 1-120 days in the future — scheduling a reminder 30 minutes before a meeting happening in 15 minutes will fail with 'time_in_past'.
Resend email templates are referenced by template ID (a UUID), not by name — 'meeting-prep' is not a valid template reference. Look up the template ID from the Resend dashboard or Templates API.

Notion date properties require ISO 8601 format with timezone — Calendly returns times in UTC. If your Notion database expects a local timezone, convert before writing or the meeting time will display incorrectly.
customer-support-ai: Discord Message Content Intent is a privileged gateway intent that must be enabled in the Discord Developer Portal — without it, message_content will be empty for messages in guilds with 100+ members, and the bot will analyze a blank question.

Anthropic API system prompts have no automatic access to your product documentation — you must include relevant FAQ/docs context as part of the system message or use RAG. Without context, the AI will hallucinate product-specific answers.

Discord rate limits are per-channel (5 messages per 5 seconds) — if multiple support questions arrive simultaneously, queue responses or the bot will be temporarily blocked with a 429 and Retry-After header.
Linear issue creation via GraphQL requires team_id as a UUID, not the team key (e.g., 'SUP') — use the teams query to resolve the key to a UUID, or the mutation returns 'Team not found'.
design-handoff: Figma webhooks only fire for the FILE_VERSION_UPDATE event when a file version is explicitly published (not on every auto-save) — instruct designers to use File > Save to Version History to trigger the workflow.

GitHub label creation is a separate API call from issue creation — if 'design-handoff' label does not exist in the repo, adding it to the issue will fail silently (the issue is created without the label, no error returned).

Linear team_id and label resolution require separate GraphQL queries — the API does not accept human-readable team names or label names directly. Cache these UUIDs in config rather than querying on every webhook.
Discord embed URLs must be HTTPS — Figma file URLs (https://figma.com/file/...) work, but if you construct URLs with http:// by mistake, Discord will not render the embed preview.
